beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check weather and star charts from last night.
beats.reasoning: Viktor's routine wake-up; he's an early riser and checks weather/sky conditions before the day. Transit strike means he'll need to drive or find alternatives.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland clinic via I-90, enjoying clear morning light on the lake.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike forces him to drive; Viktor loves the I-90 route for its scenic value anyway. He's frugal but will tolerate the commute rather than surge-priced rideshare.

beats.activity: Morning and early afternoon patient sessions at the sports clinic.
beats.reasoning: Full work block; Thursday is typically steady at the clinic. Mild weather means fewer heat-related complaints than yesterday's scorcher.

beats.activity: Lunch at the deli next to the clinic, sandwich and water.
beats.reasoning: Viktor's routine; he's frugal and consistent, hits the same deli most days. Part of his predictable work rhythm.

beats.activity: Afternoon patient sessions and admin work at the clinic.
beats.reasoning: Completes the workday; transit strike is clearing by 4pm but he'll stay at the clinic until normal end time.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle via I-90, taking scenic route and noting partly cloudy sky.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike resolved by now, but Viktor prefers driving anyway for the lake views. Extra buffer time to account for any residual traffic from the strike.

beats.activity: Stop by Goodwill on the way home to browse for thrift finds.
beats.reasoning: Viktor's habit from recent days—he's a thrifter and always takes the scenic route home. Fremont is on a natural path from I-90 back to Phinney Ridge.

beats.activity: Short walk back to car and drive final stretch home.
beats.reasoning: Completes the commute home; minimal travel gap from Fremont to Phinney Ridge.

beats.activity: Cook pasta dinner, stretch out lower back, relax on the patio.
beats.reasoning: Viktor's evening wind-down; he's frugal (cooks pasta most nights) and needs to decompress after a full day. Partly cloudy means stargazing may be compromised but he'll still spend time outside.

beats.activity: Evening on the patio with star charts, checking Jupiter and whatever else breaks through the clouds.
beats.reasoning: Viktor's favorite wind-down activity; partly cloudy won't stop him from attempting stargazing. Energy was 50/100 yesterday, so he's staying home and keeping it low-key but engaged.
7.5-hour sleep, ending at 6am the next calendar day as required. Viktor is routine-bound and will sleep well after a steady day of work and quiet evening stargazing.

diary:
Woke up early to clear skies and spent a few minutes with my star charts—Jupiter was still hanging around from last night's viewing. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, lake catching that perfect morning light. Clinic was steady; had a runner with a stubborn knee issue and a swimmer working through shoulder tension. Grabbed my usual sandwich at the deli, nothing fancy but it hit the spot.

The transit strike meant everyone was driving today, but I'd avoided the worst of it by heading out early. On the way back, I took the scenic route along I-90 and actually noticed the partly cloudy sky—sometimes you miss those details when you're rushing. Stopped by Goodwill in Fremont on impulse and found a vintage sweater that might actually fit. Cooked pasta at home, stretched out my own lower back—occupational irony—and spent the evening on the patio with my charts. Jupiter's still visible when the clouds part. Felt good, quiet, like the day had rhythm to it.
thought_process: The transit strike didn't really affect me since I drive, but I was aware of it in the back of my mind—fewer patients texted to reschedule, which meant the clinic was understaffed in a different way. I chose the scenic route home partly because I had time and partly because the morning light put me in that mood where you notice things. The Goodwill stop was unplanned but felt right; I wasn't stressed, just wandering. My back's been tight from work, so cooking and stretching felt like self-care rather than obligation. The evening on the patio was the real anchor of my day—that's where I settle, where the work and the commute fade. The weather cooperated enough for stargazing, even partial. I think I needed that quiet after a full clinic day.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
